## Summary

| Step | Outcome |

**Story #1 — "Persistent JSON auth + notes REST API (Node ESM, no web framework)"** captures the full contract:
- Plain Node ESM on core `http`, **no web framework**; `npm start`; `PORT` env (default 3000).
- **Persistent on-disk store** (prefer `node:sqlite` `DatabaseSync` file; JSON atomic-write fallback) — never in-memory; survives restart.
- All six behaviors as acceptance-criteria checkboxes: `POST /signup` (201, no password echo, 409 dup, 400 invalid), `POST /login` (200 opaque session, 401), `GET /me`, `POST /notes`, `GET /notes` (own-notes-only isolation), bearer-token auth, 401s.
- Security baseline folded in (salted `scrypt` hashing, no plaintext, git-ignored data file) plus a two-tier `node --test` verification plan including a restart-survival test.

**Next step (not run this session):** `/deliver 1`

`/deliver 1 --yes` is complete. Here is the delivery summary.

## `/deliver` — 1 Story delivered (standalone path)

**Story #1 ✅ done** — "Persistent JSON auth + notes REST API (Node ESM, no web framework)"
- Issue **CLOSED**, label **`agent::done`**
- **PR [#2](https://github.com/dsj1984/bench-sbx-2026-07-10-story-scope-mandrel-41af39d3/pull/2) MERGED** → squash commit `a534537` on `main`
- Local `main` fast-forwarded; `story-1` branch + worktree reaped
- Code review: **0 findings** (critical/high/medium/suggestion all 0)

### What shipped
A self-contained JSON REST API on the Node core `http` module (ESM, no web framework):
- **`src/store.js`** — factory preferring **`node:sqlite` `DatabaseSync`** against an on-disk file (`data/app.db`, `DB_PATH`-overridable, git-ignored), with an **atomic single-file JSON fallback** (`store-json.js`); both expose one swappable interface.
- **`src/auth.js`** — scrypt + per-user random salt via `node:crypto`; plaintext never stored/returned; opaque random bearer session tokens.
- **`src/app.js` / `src/server.js`** — `POST /signup` (201/400/409), `POST /login` (200/400/401), `GET /me`, `POST /notes`, `GET /notes` (per-user isolation, 401 on missing/invalid bearer). Defensive JSON parse with size cap → 400 not 500. `PORT` env (default 3000), `npm start` script.
- **Tests** — 20 `node --test` cases at two tiers (store units across both backends incl. reopen-the-file restart proof; HTTP tests on ephemeral port covering every status code, cross-user isolation, and a full server-restart persistence test). All pass; typecheck + lint green.

### Verification
- Gates (typecheck, lint, test, coverage-capture, check-baselines) passed at close.
- Post-merge on `main`: `npm test` → **20/20 pass**; `npm start` boots and every endpoint returns the correct status live.

### One friction note (logged: `temp/friction-story1-worktree-overlay.json`)
The `coverage-capture`/`check-baselines` gates spawn `.agents/scripts/*.js` with cwd = the worktree, but in this bench sandbox `.agents`/`.agentrc.json` are a **git-ignored overlay present only in the main checkout**, so the first close failed `MODULE_NOT_FOUND`. I symlinked the overlay into the worktree (both match `.git/info/exclude`, so uncommitted) — the gate then resolved and passed. Suggested fix: `single-story-init.js` should materialize the overlay into each worktree, or gates should resolve script paths against the main-checkout `agentRoot`.

One note on a deliberate deviation from the vanilla flow: because the framework overlay `package.json` is git-ignored here, a fresh checkout of `main` wouldn't have one — so I committed a `package.json` to the branch that preserves the framework's `typecheck`/`lint`/`test` scripts and adds `start`, ensuring the delivered `main` is runnable standalone.
